author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XI. 55–57; XII.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yesterday’s lesson in the holy Gospel, on which we spake as the Lord enabled us, is followed by to-day’s, on which we purpose to speak in the same spirit of dependence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some passages in the Scriptures are so clear as to require a hearer rather than an expounder: over such we need not tarry, that we may have sufficient time for those which necessarily demand a fuller consideration.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the Jews’ passover was nigh at hand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews wished to have that feast-day crimsoned with the blood of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On it that Lamb was slain, who hath consecrated it as a feast-day for us by His own blood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There was a plot among the Jews about slaying Jesus: and He, who had come from heaven to suffer, wished to draw near to the place of His suffering, because the hour of His passion was at hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “many went out of the country up to Jerusalem before the passover, to sanctify themselves.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews did so in accordance with the command of the Lord delivered by holy Moses in the law, that on the feast-day of the passover all should assemble from every part of the land, and be sanctified in celebrating the services of the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that celebration was a shadow of the future.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why a shadow?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was a prophetic intimation of the Christ to come, a prophecy of Him who on that day was to suffer for us: that so the shadow might vanish and the light come; that the sign might pass away, and the truth be retained.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews therefore held the passover in a shadowy form, but we in the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what need was there that the Lord should command them to slay a sheep on the very day of the feast, save only because of Him it was prophesied, “He is led as a sheep to the slaughter”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The door-posts of the Jews were sealed with the blood of the slaughtered animal: with the blood of Christ are our foreheads sealed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that sealing—for it had a real significance—was said to keep away the destroyer from the houses that were sealed: Christ’s seal drives away the destroyer from us, if we receive the Saviour into our hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why have I said this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because many have their door-posts sealed while there is no inmate abiding within: they find it easy to have Christ’s seal in the forehead, and yet at heart refuse admission to His word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, I have said, and I repeat it, Christ’s seal driveth from us the destroyer, if only we have Christ as an inmate of our hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have stated these things, lest any one’s thoughts should be turning on the meaning of these festivals of the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord therefore came as it were to the victim’s place, that the true passover might be ours, when we celebrated His passion as the real offering of the lamb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then sought they for Jesus:” but with evil intent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For happy are they who seek for Jesus in a way that is good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They sought for Him, with the intent that neither they nor we should have Him more: but in departing from them, He has been received by us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some who seek Him are blamed, others who do so are commended; for it is the spirit animating the seeker that finds either praise or condemnation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thence you have it also in the psalms, “Let them be confounded and put to shame that seek after my soul:” such are those who sought with evil purpose.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in another place he says, “Refuge hath failed me, and there is no one that seeketh after my soul.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who sought, and those who did not, are blamed alike.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore let us seek for Christ, that He may be ours, that we may keep Him, and not that we may slay Him; for these men sought to get hold of Him, but only for the purpose of speedily getting quit of Him for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore they sought for Him, and spake among themselves: What think ye, that He will not come to the feast?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where He were, he should show it, that they might take Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us for our parts show the Jews where Christ is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would, indeed, that all the seed of those who had given commandment to have it shown them where Christ was, would but hear and apprehend!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them come to the church and hear where Christ is, and take Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They may hear it from us, they may hear it from the gospel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was slain by their forefathers, He was buried, He rose again, He was recognized by the disciples, He ascended before their eyes into heaven, and there sitteth at the right hand of the Father; and He who was judged is yet to come as Judge of all: let them hear, and hold fast.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do they reply, How shall I take hold of the absent? how shall I stretch up my hand into heaven, and take hold of one who is sitting there?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Stretch up thy faith, and thou hast got hold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy forefathers held by the flesh, hold thou with the heart; for the absent Christ is also present.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But for His presence, we ourselves were unable to hold Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since His word is true, “Lo, I am with you alway, even to the end of the world,” He is away, and He is here; He has returned, and will not forsake us; for He has carried His body into heaven, but His majesty He has never withdrawn from the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then Jesus, six days before the passover, came to Bethany, where Lazarus was who had been dead, whom Jesus raised from the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there they made Him a supper; and Martha served: but Lazarus was one of them that reclined at the table.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To prevent people thinking that the man had become a phantom, because he had risen from the dead, he was one of those who reclined at table; he was living, speaking, feasting: the truth was made manifest, and the unbelief of the Jews was confounded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, therefore, reclined at table with Lazarus and the others; and they were waited on by Martha, one of the sisters of Lazarus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But “Mary,” the other sister of Lazarus, “took a pound of ointment of pure nard, very precious, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped His feet with her hair; and the house was filled with the odor of the ointment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such was the incident, let us look into the mystery it imported.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever soul of you wishes to be truly faithful, anoint like Mary the feet of the Lord with precious ointment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That ointment was righteousness, and therefore it was [exactly] a pound weight: but it was ointment of pure nard [nardi pistici], very precious.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From his calling it “pistici,” we ought to infer that there was some locality from which it derived its preciousness: but this does not exhaust its meaning, and it harmonizes well with a sacramental symbol.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The root of the word [“pure”] in the Greek is by us called “faith.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wert seeking to work righteousness: the just shall live by faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Anoint the feet of Jesus: follow by a good life the Lord’s footsteps.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wipe them with thy hair: what thou hast of superfluity, give to the poor, and thou hast wiped the feet of the Lord; for the hair seems to be the superfluous part of the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast something to spare of thy abundance: it is superfluous to thee, but necessary for the feet of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps on this earth the Lord’s feet are still in need.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of whom but of His members is He yet to say in the end, “Inasmuch as ye did it to one of the least of mine, ye did it unto me”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye spent what was superfluous for yourselves, but ye have done what was grateful to my feet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the house was filled with the odor.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The world is filled with the fame of a good character: for a good character is as a pleasant odor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who live wickedly and bear the name of Christians, do injury to Christ: of such it is said, that through them “the name of the Lord is blasphemed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If through such God’s name is blasphemed, through the good the name of the Lord is honored.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the apostle, when he says, “We are a sweet savor of Christ in every place.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As it is said also in the Song of Songs, “Thy name is as ointment poured forth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Attend again to the apostle: “We are a sweet savor,” he says, “of Christ in every place, both in them that are saved, and in them that perish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the one we are the savor of life unto life, to the other the savor of death unto death: and who is sufficient for these things?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The lesson of the holy Gospel before us affords us the opportunity of so speaking of that savor, that we on our part may give worthy utterance, and you diligent heed, to what is thus expressed by the apostle himself, “And who is sufficient for these things?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But have we any reason to infer from these words that we are qualified to attempt speaking on such a subject, or you to hear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We, indeed, are not so; but He is sufficient, who is pleased to speak by us what it may be for your profit to hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle, you see, is, as he calls himself, “a sweet savor:” but that sweet savor is “to some the savor of life unto life, and to others the savor of death unto death;” and yet all the while “a sweet savor” in itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he does not say, does he, To some we are a sweet savor unto life, to others an evil savor unto death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He called himself a sweet savor, not an evil; and represented himself as the same sweet savor, to some unto life, to others unto death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Happy they who find life in this sweet savor! but what misery can be greater than theirs, to whom the sweet savor is the messenger of death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is it, says some one, that is thus slain by the sweet savor?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is to this the apostle alludes in the words, “And who is sufficient for these things?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what wonderful ways God brings it about that the good savor is fraught both with life to the good, and with death to the wicked; how it is so, so far as the Lord is pleased to inspire my thoughts (for it may still conceal a deeper meaning beyond my power to penetrate),—yet so far, I say, as my power of penetration has reached, you ought not to have the information withheld.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The integrity of the Apostle Paul’s life and conduct, his preaching of righteousness in word and exhibition of it in works, his wondrous power as a teacher and his fidelity as a steward, were everywhere noised abroad: he was loved by some, and envied by others.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he himself tells us in a certain place of some, that they preached Christ not sincerely, but of envy; “thinking,” he says, “to add affliction to my bonds.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what does he add?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whether in pretence or in truth, let Christ be preached.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They preach who love me, they preach who hate me; in that good savor the former live, in it the others die: and yet by the preaching of both let the name of Christ be proclaimed, with this excellent savor let the world be filled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou been loving one whose conduct evidenced his goodness then in this good savor thou hast lived.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou been envying such a one? then in this same savor thou hast died.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But hast thou, pray, in thus choosing to die, converted this savor into an evil one?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Turn from thine envious feelings, and the good savor will cease to slay thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now, lastly, listen to what we have here, how this ointment was to some a sweet savor unto life, and to others a sweet savor unto death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the pious Mary had rendered this grateful service to the Lord, straightway one of His disciples, Judas Iscariot, who was yet to betray Him, said, “Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence, and given to the poor?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Alas for thee, wretched man! the sweet savor hath slain thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the cause that led him so to speak is disclosed by the holy evangelist.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we, too, might have supposed, had not the real state of his mind been revealed in the Gospel, that the care of the poor might have induced him so to speak.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hearken to a true witness: “This he said, not that he cared for the poor; but because he was a thief, and had the money bag, and bare what was put therein.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did he bear it about, or bear it away?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the common service he bore it, as a thief he bore it away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look now, and learn that this Judas did not become perverted only at the time when he yielded to the bribery of the Jews and betrayed his Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For not a few, inattentive to the Gospel, suppose that Judas only perished when he accepted money from the Jews to betray the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was not then that he perished, but he was already a thief, and a reprobate, when following the Lord; for it was with his body and not with his heart that he followed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He made up the apostolic number of twelve, but had no part in the apostolic blessedness: he had been made the twelfth in semblance, and on his departure, and the succession of another, the apostolic reality was completed, and the entireness of the number conserved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What lesson then, my brethren, did our Lord Jesus Christ wish to impress on His Church, when it pleased Him to have one castaway among the twelve, but this, that we should bear with the wicked, and refrain from dividing the body of Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here you have Judas among the saints,—that Judas, mark you! who was a thief, yea—do not overlook it—not a thief of any ordinary type, but a thief and a sacrilegist: a robber of money bags, but of such as were the Lord’s; of money bags, but of such as were sacred.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If there is a distinction made in the public courts between such crimes as ordinary theft and peculation,—for by peculation we mean the theft of public property; and private theft is not visited with the same sentence as public,—how much more severe ought to be the sentence on the sacrilegious thief, who has dared to steal, not from places of any ordinary kind, but to steal from the Church?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who thieves from the Church, stands side by side with the castaway Judas.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such was this man Judas, and yet he went in and out with the eleven holy disciples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With them he came even to the table of the Lord: he was permitted to have intercourse with them, but he could not contaminate them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of one bread did both Peter and Judas partake, and yet what communion had the believer with the infidel?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter’s partaking was unto life, but that of Judas unto death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that good bread was just like the sweet savor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as the sweet savor, so also does the good bread give life to the good, and bring death to the wicked.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For he that eateth unworthily, eateth and drinketh judgment to himself:” “judgment to himself,” not to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, it is judgment to himself, not to thee, bear as one that is good with him that is evil, that thou mayest attain unto the rewards of the good, and be not hurled into the punishment of the wicked.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lay to heart our Lord’s example while living with man upon earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why had He a money bag, who was ministered unto by angels, save to intimate that His Church was destined thereafter to have her repository for money?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why gave He admission to a thief, save to teach His Church patiently to bear with thieves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he who had formed the habit of abstracting money from the bag, did not hesitate for money received to sell the Lord Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let us see what answer our Lord gave to such words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, brethren: He does not say to him, Thou speakest so on account of thy thievishness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He knew him to be a thief, yet did not betray him, but rather endured him, and showed us an example of patience in tolerating the wicked in the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then said Jesus to him: Let her keep it against the day of my burial.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He announced that His own death was at hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what follows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the poor ye have always with you, but me ye will not have always.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We can certainly understand, “the poor ye have always;” what He has thus said is true.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When were the poor wanting in the Church?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But me ye will not have always;” what does He mean by this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How are we to understand, “Me ye will not have always”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Don’t be alarmed: it was addressed to Judas.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, did He not say, thou wilt have, but, ye will have?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because Judas is not here a unit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One wicked man represents the whole body of the wicked; in the same way as Peter, the whole body of the good, yea, the body of the Church, but in respect to the good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if in Peter’s case there were no sacramental symbol of the Church, the Lord would not have said to him, “I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven; and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If this was said only to Peter, it gives no ground of action to the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if such is the case also in the Church, that what is bound on earth is bound in heaven, and what is loosed on earth is loosed in heaven,—for when the Church excommunicates, the excommunicated person is bound in heaven; when one is reconciled by the Church, the person so reconciled is loosed in heaven:—if such, then, is the case in the Church, Peter, in receiving the keys, represented the holy Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, in the person of Peter were represented the good in the Church, and in Judas’ person were represented the bad in the Church, then to these latter was it said, “But me ye will not have always.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what means the “not always;” and what, the “always”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou art good, if thou belongest to the body represented by Peter, thou hast Christ both now and hereafter: now by faith, by sign, by the sacrament of baptism, by the bread and wine of the altar.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast Christ now, but thou wilt have Him always; for when thou hast gone hence, thou wilt come to Him who said to the robber, “To-day shalt thou be with me in paradise.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou livest wickedly, thou mayest seem to have Christ now, because thou enterest the Church, signest thyself with the sign of Christ, art baptized with the baptism of Christ, minglest thyself with the members of Christ, and approachest His altar: now thou hast Christ, but by living wickedly thou wilt not have Him always.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It may be also understood in this way: “The poor ye will have always with you, but me ye will not have always.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The good may take it also as addressed to themselves, but not so as to be any source of anxiety; for He was speaking of His bodily presence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in respect of His majesty, His providence, His ineffable and invisible grace, His own words are fulfilled, “Lo, I am with you alway, even to the end of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in respect of the flesh He assumed as the Word, in respect of that which He was as the son of the Virgin, of that wherein He was seized by the Jews, nailed to the tree, let down from the cross, enveloped in a shroud, laid in the sepulchre, and manifested in His resurrection, “ye will not have Him always.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because in respect of His bodily presence He associated for forty days with His disciples, and then, having brought them forth for the purpose of beholding and not of following Him, He ascended into heaven, and is no longer here.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is there, indeed, sitting at the right hand of the Father; and He is here also, having never withdrawn the presence of His glory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In other words, in respect of His divine presence we always have Christ; in respect of His presence in the flesh it was rightly said to the disciples, “Me ye will not have always.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this respect the Church enjoyed His presence only for a few days: now it possesses Him by faith, without seeing Him with the eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In whichever way, then, it was said, “But me ye will not have always,” it can no longer, I suppose, after this twofold solution, remain as a subject of doubt.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us listen to the other few points that remain: “Much people of the Jews therefore knew that He was there: and they came not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus, whom He had raised from the dead.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were drawn by curiosity, not by charity: they came and saw.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hearken to the strange scheming of human vanity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Having seen Lazarus as one raised from the dead,—for the fame of such a miracle of the Lord’s had been accompanied everywhere with so much evidence of its genuineness, and it had been so openly performed, that they could neither conceal nor deny what had been done,—only think of the plan they hit upon.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But the chief priests consulted that they might put Lazarus also to death; because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O foolish consultation and blinded rage!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Could not Christ the Lord, who was able to raise the dead, raise also the slain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When you were preparing a violent death for Lazarus, were you at the same time denuding the Lord of His power?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you think a dead man one thing, a murdered man another, look you only to this, that the Lord made both, and raised Lazarus to life when dead, and Himself when slain.
